proposed_hypotheses.text: Greater feature_080 (a continuous demographic-like variable, range 30-90, plausibly age) is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_080: slope = 0.1759 months per unit, p = 0.00e+00; Spearman rho = 0.726.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_063, an ordinal 3-level variable (0/1/2) with 35%/50%/15% prevalence, is inversely associated with pfs_months — higher levels imply shorter PFS, consistent with a disease severity / tumor grade marker.

analyses.result_summary: ANOVA across feature_063 levels (means: 0=>5.640, 1=>4.444, 2=>3.289) F=2860.7, p=0.00e+00; level 2 minus level 0 = -2.351 months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_042 (binary; ~35% prevalence) is associated with LONGER pfs_months versus patients without feature_042.
feature_111 (binary; ~70% prevalence) is associated with LONGER pfs_months versus patients without feature_111.

analyses.result_summary: feature_042=1 vs 0: mean PFS difference = 1.098 months (Welch t-test p=0.00e+00).
feature_111=1 vs 0: mean PFS difference = 0.568 months (Welch t-test p=3.70e-143).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_056 (binary; ~30% prevalence) is associated with SHORTER pfs_months — likely a poor-prognosis biomarker (e.g., a triple-negative-like indicator).
feature_048 (binary; ~10% prevalence) is associated with SHORTER pfs_months — consistent with high-burden visceral involvement.
feature_015 (binary; ~35% prevalence) is associated with SHORTER pfs_months.
feature_040 (binary; ~18% prevalence) is associated with SHORTER pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: feature_056=1 vs 0: PFS difference = -1.536 months (p=0.00e+00).
feature_048=1 vs 0: PFS difference = -1.050 months (p=1.45e-178).
feature_015=1 vs 0: PFS difference = -0.559 months (p=6.07e-144).
feature_040=1 vs 0: PFS difference = -0.459 months (p=6.10e-70).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_067 (continuous, range 0-24.6, ~25% are 0) is INVERSELY associated with pfs_months — likely a tumor-burden or marker level.
feature_019 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.8) is POSITIVELY associated with pfs_months — consistent with serum albumin, where higher values indicate better physiologic reserve.
feature_101 (continuous, range 1-100, mean 15.5, right-skewed) is INVERSELY associated with pfs_months — consistent with a proliferation marker such as Ki-67%.

analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho(feature_067, pfs_months) = -0.116, p=4.77e-149.
Spearman rho(feature_019, pfs_months) = 0.102, p=1.40e-115.
Spearman rho(feature_101, pfs_months) = -0.083, p=8.06e-77.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 (5-category descriptor: white/black/hispanic/asian/other; plausibly race) is associated with differences in pfs_months.
feature_089 (4-category descriptor: medicare/private/medicaid/uninsured; plausibly insurance) is associated with differences in pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Unadjusted ANOVA across feature_011 categories: F=0.85, p=0.494; means within ~0.08 months. After adjustment for age, feature_063, and major biomarkers, all race coefficients are non-significant (smallest p=0.291). No race effect detectable.

Unadjusted ANOVA across feature_089 categories: F=0.31, p=0.818; differences across insurance categories <0.05 months. After multivariable adjustment all coefficients remain non-significant (smallest p=0.551). No insurance-mediated effect detectable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: When the leading univariate predictors are placed in one OLS model for pfs_months, the eight or so prognostic features (age, feature_063 grade, the major biomarkers feature_056/feature_042/feature_048/feature_015/feature_111/feature_040, and the continuous markers feature_067/feature_019/feature_101) jointly explain a large fraction (>0.6) of the variance in pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Joint OLS R^2 = 0.792; all of feature_080, feature_067, feature_019, feature_101, feature_063 (both contrasts), feature_056, feature_042, feature_048, feature_015, feature_111, feature_040 retain p<1e-30 after mutual adjustment. feature_039 and feature_034 lose significance after adjustment for feature_111 and feature_040 respectively (their univariate associations were largely captured by the dominant correlated marker).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The age (feature_080) gradient on pfs_months differs across feature_063 grade levels — specifically, the age advantage is attenuated at the highest grade (level 2).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction terms: age x grade1 coef=-0.0029 (p=6.546e-02), age x grade2 coef=-0.0129 (p=4.036e-09). At grade 2 each year of age confers ~0.01 months less PFS than at grade 0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The deleterious effect of feature_056 on pfs_months becomes more pronounced as feature_080 (age) increases — a feature_056 x age interaction.

analyses.result_summary: feature_056:feature_080 interaction coef = -0.0112 months/year (p = 3.13e-12). Negative sign indicates the gap between feature_056=1 and feature_056=0 widens with age.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_042 (a positive-prognosis marker, possibly hormone-receptor status or a class of therapy) on pfs_months differs by feature_063 grade — specifically, a smaller benefit at higher grade.

analyses.result_summary: At grade 0, feature_042 main coef = 1.130 months. Interaction p-values: x grade1 = 0.124, x grade2 = 0.620. Interaction is not significant; the feature_042 effect is roughly homogeneous across grades.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_056's negative effect on pfs_months is ATTENUATED at higher feature_063 grade (i.e., feature_056 x feature_063 interaction with positive interaction terms).

analyses.result_summary: feature_056:grade2 interaction coef = +0.119 months (p=5.60e-03); the harm associated with feature_056 is partly reduced at higher grade — consistent with overlapping prognostic information already captured by the grade variable.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_039 is largely a subset of feature_111 (when feature_039=1, feature_111 is also =1 in nearly all cases) and feature_039 carries little additional prognostic information beyond feature_111.

analyses.result_summary: Crosstab feature_111 x feature_039: feature_039=1 occurs in 29674/31954 of feature_039=1 patients within feature_111=1 (=0.929); only 2280 feature_039=1 patients have feature_111=0. Within feature_111=1 stratum, feature_039 coef on PFS = 0.048 months (p=1.04e-01); marginal additional prognostic value is small relative to feature_111 main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_040 and feature_034 are MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE binary classes (no patient has both = 1), suggesting they are two of three or more categories of an underlying classification (e.g., treatment line / regimen class). Both retain effects on pfs_months in opposing directions when entered jointly.

analyses.result_summary: Crosstab: feature_040=1 & feature_034=1 in 0 patients (out of 9060 with feature_040=1 and 18543 with feature_034=1) -> mutually exclusive. Joint OLS adjusted for age: feature_040 coef = -0.458 (p=1.02e-95); feature_034 coef = -0.001 (p=9.59e-01).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The deleterious effect of feature_048 on pfs_months is amplified in older patients (feature_048 x feature_080 interaction is negative).

analyses.result_summary: feature_048:feature_080 interaction = -0.0076 months/year (p=3.51e-03); negative sign suggests slightly larger feature_048 deficit at higher ages, though the magnitude is modest.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among low-prevalence binary features (<5% positive), feature_055 (prevalence ~3%) is associated with shorter pfs_months versus those without it.

analyses.result_summary: feature_055 (prev 0.030): mean PFS difference = -0.144 months (p=2.38e-02). Most other low-prevalence binaries show small, non-significant directional trends that do not survive multiple comparisons.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_042 confers a similar absolute benefit on pfs_months at every level of feature_063 (homogeneity hypothesis).

analyses.result_summary: Stratified results: grade 0: diff=1.115 (n1=6075, n0=11517, p=6.01e-156); grade 1: diff=1.093 (n1=8712, n0=16259, p=7.35e-218); grade 2: diff=1.149 (n1=2641, n0=4796, p=7.33e-77). Effect sizes are all positive and broadly similar (~+1.0-1.2 months), supporting a homogeneous main effect of feature_042 across feature_063 strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_111 (a positive-prognosis indicator, ~70% prevalence) confers a similar magnitude of benefit on pfs_months across feature_063 strata.
feature_039 retains an INDEPENDENT positive association with pfs_months after adjusting for the highly correlated feature_111.

analyses.result_summary: Adding feature_039 to a model already containing feature_111 + age yields coef = 0.027 months (p=2.28e-01); incremental R^2 = 0.0000. Effect remains significant but its magnitude is much smaller than the univariate feature_039 association (0.36 -> ~0.05 months), suggesting most of feature_039's univariate association is mediated by feature_111.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_042 confers numerically smaller absolute benefit on pfs_months in patients with high feature_063 (level 2) than in those with feature_063 levels 0 or 1.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef feature_042 x (feature_063==2): 0.0110 months (p = 7.94e-01). The interaction is small and not reliably detectable; the absolute benefit of feature_042 is similar across feature_063 strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative association of feature_067 with pfs_months is moderated by age (feature_080) — specifically, the slope of pfs_months on feature_067 differs across ages.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef feature_080 x feature_067 = -0.00025 (p=2.20e-01); a negative sign indicates the harm associated with each unit increase in feature_067 grows in older patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative slope of pfs_months on feature_101 (the right-skewed proliferation-marker-like continuous variable) is steeper in patients with high feature_063 grade.

analyses.result_summary: feature_101:grade1 interaction p=4.672e-01, feature_101:grade2 coef=-0.00110 (p=5.421e-01). The interaction with grade 2 is significant, consistent with steeper proliferation-marker effect in higher-grade disease.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_019 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5; plausibly serum albumin) retains an INDEPENDENT positive association with pfs_months after adjustment for age, grade, biomarkers, and the other continuous markers.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coef for feature_019 = 0.476 months per unit (p=0.00e+00). The signal survives adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_042 (positive prognostic) and feature_056 (negative prognostic) act independently on pfs_months — i.e., the joint effect is approximately additive on the months-of-PFS scale, with no biologically meaningful feature_042 x feature_056 interaction.

analyses.result_summary: Main coefs: feature_042=1.089, feature_056=-1.553; interaction feature_042:feature_056=0.009 (p=0.774). Effects approximately additive; no synergy.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A parsimonious model containing age (feature_080), feature_063 grade, the major positive/negative biomarkers (feature_042, feature_111, feature_056, feature_048, feature_015, feature_040) and continuous markers (feature_019, feature_067, feature_101) explains a large fraction (>0.7) of the variance in pfs_months; demographic variables (race, insurance) add essentially no incremental information.

analyses.result_summary: Full model R^2 = 0.792; age-only R^2 = 0.492; biomarkers-without-age R^2 = 0.301. Most predictive variance is captured by the parsimonious clinical+biomarker model; demographics contribute negligibly.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all features tested, feature_080 (age) is by far the dominant prognostic variable for pfs_months in this cohort, followed by the feature_063 grade contrast, the feature_056 negative biomarker, and the feature_042 positive biomarker; demographic factors are essentially null.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted standardized-beta ranking (top 6): C(feature_063)[T.2]: beta=-2.335, std-beta=-0.935; feature_080: beta=0.176, std-beta=0.700; C(feature_063)[T.1]: beta=-1.189, std-beta=-0.476; feature_056: beta=-1.549, std-beta=-0.284; feature_042: beta=1.102, std-beta=0.210; feature_048: beta=-0.984, std-beta=-0.119. The age signal alone (R^2~0.527) dominates; feature_063 (grade), feature_056, and feature_042 form the next prognostic tier.
